summary.one_liner: Toy yield vault with two unprivileged fund-drain paths (public emergency drain and public oracle), deposit math that panics on the first deposit, and a pause that does not cover withdrawals — illustrative fixture, do not deploy.
asset_inventory: share token: minted on deposit (src/lib.rs:38, src/lib.rs:90)
external_dependencies: none — the oracle is an internal struct field, not an external component (src/lib.rs:40)

findings.title: emergency_drain is public and unbounded
findings.what: emergency_drain is declared PUBLIC (src/lib.rs:30) and calls self.vault.take_all() (src/lib.rs:120), so any caller can empty the vault in one transaction.
findings.why: Total, immediate loss of all deposited XRD, by anyone.
findings.suggested_direction: Gate behind admin or a dedicated owner role and bound it with a per-call cap and epoch cooldown rather than draining everything at once.

findings.title: set_oracle_price is public, single-source, and unchecked
findings.what: Anyone can call set_oracle_price (src/lib.rs:29) to set any price with no staleness or sanity bound, then deposit/withdraw against it in the same transaction.
findings.why: An attacker sets a favourable price, mints or redeems shares, and extracts value — a second drain path independent of F-001.
findings.suggested_direction: Make price updates privileged, source from a real multi-source oracle, and add staleness and deviation bounds before a price is usable.

findings.title: deposit share math divides by zero and can overflow
findings.what: shares = amount * price / total_value * total_shares uses raw operators; on the first deposit total_value is 0 (src/lib.rs:86) so the division panics, and the chained multiply can overflow Decimal.
findings.why: The first deposit always reverts (the vault cannot be bootstrapped), and the formula is wrong even when it does not panic.
findings.suggested_direction: Special-case the first deposit, use checked_mul/checked_div, and define an explicit share formula with documented vault-favouring rounding.

findings.title: withdraw ignores the pause flag
findings.what: withdraw never checks self.paused (src/lib.rs:95-101), so withdrawals proceed while the vault is paused.
findings.why: If pause does not cover exits it cannot contain an incident, and it leaves F-002's price-manipulation drain unstoppable.
findings.suggested_direction: Assert !self.paused at the top of withdraw and any other value-moving method so the circuit breaker covers exits.

findings.title: unpause epoch check is off by one
findings.what: The assert requires current_epoch > unlock_epoch (src/lib.rs:132) — one epoch — but the message and intent promise two full epochs.
findings.why: The minimum-pause window is half what is documented, weakening the delay the brake is meant to provide.
findings.suggested_direction: Compare against unlock_epoch + 2 and add an at-boundary test so the enforced delay matches intent.

findings.title: admin can rotate itself and there is no owner
findings.what: admin => updatable_by: [admin] (src/lib.rs:23) lets the admin role rewrite its own rule, and the component globalizes with OwnerRole::None (src/lib.rs:72).
findings.why: A compromised or fat-fingered admin is unrecoverable; there is no owner to rotate or freeze it.
findings.suggested_direction: Introduce an owner role that governs admin, and make admin updatable_by the owner only.

findings.title: set_fee_bps has no upper bound
findings.what: set_fee_bps stores bps directly (src/lib.rs:106) with no range check, so the fee can be set above 100%.
findings.why: A bad or malicious fee can make the vault unusable or silently expropriate depositors.
findings.suggested_direction: Assert an explicit ceiling (e.g. bps <= dec!(1000)) and reject out-of-range values.

findings.title: no events on state-changing methods
findings.what: deposit, withdraw, set_oracle_price, and emergency_drain mutate state but emit no events (src/lib.rs:82-121).
findings.why: Off-chain indexers and monitors cannot observe deposits, redemptions, price changes, or drains, which also hampers incident detection for F-001/F-002.
findings.suggested_direction: Define and emit an event for each state change, including amounts and the resource address.

checklist_coverage.note: every bucket is deposited, burned, or returned
no extern_blueprint!, Global<X> calls, or CALL_METHOD
deposit/withdraw take no caller min_out against the mutable price
no long-lived proofs or access-rule grants handed out

pattern_conformance.note: only a self-rotating admin with OwnerRole::None (src/lib.rs:23, src/lib.rs:72)

pattern_conformance.pattern: Per-call cap + cooldown on emergency withdraw
pattern_conformance.note: emergency_drain takes the whole vault in one call (src/lib.rs:120)
single mutable price field, no freshness or deviation check (src/lib.rs:40, src/lib.rs:111)
paused flag exists but withdraw ignores it (src/lib.rs:95)

test_coverage_gaps: withdraw has zero tests — tests/vault.rs exercises only deposit (tests/vault.rs:13)
no auth-violation test for set_fee_bps/pause/unpause (negative path untested)
no 'withdraw while paused' test (would catch F-004)
set_oracle_price and emergency_drain have no tests at all
open_questions: Is set_oracle_price public by intent (e.g. a permissionless keeper)? It still needs staleness and sanity bounds before use (src/lib.rs:111).
Should deposit/withdraw accept a caller min_shares_out / min_amount_out to resist sandwiching given the mutable price (src/lib.rs:82)?
Is a governance/owner layer above admin intended? OwnerRole::None leaves no recovery path if the admin badge is lost or compromised (src/lib.rs:72).
